title: T2: Scan-time narrow path anchoring per builder type
description: Differentiate narrow vs broad output per scan-builder type so fresh-repo PreToolUse hook is no longer silent. Currently all scan builders emit broad+[] which makes the narrow-path PreToolUse injector ineffective at cold-start. Refactor: tech-stack / module-structure / build-config / code-style / CI-config builders emit narrow with mechanically-known relevance_paths; only readme-first-paragraph stays broad. Preserves Q-20 (LLM-import path stays broad+[]); ONLY scan-time output is auto-narrowed because paths are deterministic.

files.change: emit `scope: narrow, relevance_paths: [matched tsconfig.json / vite.config.* / build script locations discovered during scan]`
explicitly emit `scope: broad, relevance_paths: []` (no change semantically, just make it explicit) — Phase 1.5 blacklist still applies
update snapshot tests for each builder to assert new narrow/broad + relevance_paths contract

implementation: Audit each builder's current output to confirm baseline (most currently emit broad+[])
For each narrow builder: hardcode the relevance_paths array since file discovery is mechanical (no LLM judgment needed); validate paths exist via existing scan-phase file detection
For readme-first-paragraph: keep broad, add explicit comment 'broad by design — single repo-root file, Phase 1.5 blacklist applies'
Update each builder's snapshot test fixtures to capture the new (scope, relevance_paths) shape
Verify scan-end events.jsonl entries reflect new scope; confirm fresh-repo PreToolUse hook fires on tsconfig/eslint/package.json edits

reference.pattern: Existing builder signature: scan-phase emits {slug, layer, scope, relevance_paths, body}; PreToolUse hook matches edit_path against entries' relevance_paths globs
reference.examples: tech-stack today emits scope: broad → change to narrow + 2 hardcoded paths

criteria: GIVEN fresh repo WHEN `fabric init` scan completes THEN canonical entries from tech-stack/module-structure/build-config/code-style/ci-config builders all have `scope: narrow` AND non-empty `relevance_paths`
GIVEN tech-stack entry with relevance_paths ['package.json'] WHEN user edits package.json THEN PreToolUse hook injects that entry's body into stderr
GIVEN readme-first-paragraph entry WHEN inspected THEN `scope: broad, relevance_paths: []`
All builder snapshot tests pass with the new contract

rationale.chosen_approach: Per-builder narrow/broad classification — each builder declares its own scope because path-known-ness is a property of what the builder produces
alternatives_considered: Single config-driven mapping in scan orchestrator
Heuristic auto-narrow based on body content
decision_factors: Locality: each builder owns its semantics
Mechanical knowledge — these paths are deterministic, not LLM judgment
rationale.tradeoffs: If a new builder is added, author must consciously decide narrow vs broad — accepted (forcing function for correctness)

manual_checks: Run fabric init on a fresh repo → inspect canonical/ entries → verify 4/5 narrow with relevance_paths populated
Edit package.json → verify PreToolUse hook fires with tech-stack content
success_metrics: ≥4 of 6 scan builders emit narrow scope
PreToolUse hook fires on ≥80% of edits to detected config files
unit: Each builder test asserts (scope, relevance_paths) shape
integration: Fresh-repo scan → PreToolUse hook injection on canonical config file edit
